basic_details.job_title: SAIL DSP Recruitment 2026 - Walkin for 11 Consultant (GDMO/Specialist) Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Steel Authority of India Limited, Durgapur Steel Plant (SAIL DSP)
basic_details.short_information: Steel Authority of India Limited, Durgapur Steel Plant (SAIL DSP) has released an official notification for Walk-in Interview for 11 Consultant (GDMO/Specialist) Posts. Eligible candidates can attend the Walk-in Interview on 21-07-2026 to 22 July 2026 at the SAIL DSP office with original documents and self-attested copies.

age_limit.details: Maximum Age Limit: 69 years for all posts. Age relaxation rules are not specified in the provided data.

qualification.education_qualification: MBBS, MBBS with PG Degree in Surgery/General Surgery, MBBS with PG Degree/PG Diploma in Paediatrics, MBBS with PG Degree / PG Diploma in Ophthalmology, MBBS with PG Degree/PG Diploma in Orthopaedics
qualification.details: GDMO: MBBS Specialist (Surgery): MBBS with PG Degree in Surgery/General Surgery Specialist (Paediatrics): MBBS with PG Degree/PG Diploma in Paediatrics Specialist (Ophthalmology): MBBS with PG Degree / PG Diploma in Ophthalmology Specialist (Orthopaedic): MBBS with PG Degree/PG Diploma in Orthopaedics

salary_details.details: GDMO: Rs. 90,000/- per month Specialist (Surgery): Rs. 1,60,000/- per month Specialist (Paediatrics): Rs. 1,20,000/- to Rs. 1,60,000/- per month Specialist (Ophthalmology): Rs. 1,20,000/- to Rs. 1,60,000/- per month Specialist (Orthopaedic): Rs. 1,20,000/- to Rs. 1,60,000/- per month

rows.steel_authority_of_india_limited_durgapu: MBBS, MBBS with PG Degree in Surgery/General Surgery, MBBS with PG Degree/PG Diploma in Paediatrics, MBBS with PG Degree / PG Diploma in Ophthalmology, MBBS with PG Degree/PG Diploma in Orthopaedics

content_sections.heading: SAIL DSP Vacancy 2026 Notification PDF
content_sections.text: Candidates should download and read the SAIL DSP Jobs 2026 notification PDF carefully to check vacancy details, eligibility, age limit, and application instructions. The notification also includes important dates and selection process details. The direct download link is available in the Important Links section below. Download SAIL DSP Job 2026 Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: SAIL DSP Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: GDMO: MBBS Specialist (Surgery): MBBS with PG Degree in Surgery/General Surgery Specialist (Paediatrics): MBBS with PG Degree/PG Diploma in Paediatrics Specialist (Ophthalmology): MBBS with PG Degree / PG Diploma in Ophthalmology Specialist (Orthopaedic): MBBS with PG Degree/PG Diploma in Orthopaedics

content_sections.heading: SAIL DSP Recruitment 2026 - Age Limit
content_sections.text: Maximum Age Limit: 69 years for all posts. Age relaxation rules are not specified in the provided data.

content_sections.heading: SAIL DSP Recruitment 2026 - Salary / Stipend
content_sections.text: GDMO: Rs. 90,000/- per month Specialist (Surgery): Rs. 1,60,000/- per month Specialist (Paediatrics): Rs. 1,20,000/- to Rs. 1,60,000/- per month Specialist (Ophthalmology): Rs. 1,20,000/- to Rs. 1,60,000/- per month Specialist (Orthopaedic): Rs. 1,20,000/- to Rs. 1,60,000/- per month

content_sections.heading: Walk-In Procedure
content_sections.text: Candidates who wish to attend the walk-in interview for SAIL DSP Various Posts Recruitment 2026 should follow the steps below. Read the official notification carefully before attending the interview. Candidates must report at the venue as per the reporting time mentioned in the notification. Carry all original documents along with one set of self-attested photocopies such as ID proof, educational certificates, experience certificates (if required), and category certificate (if applicable). Bring the duly filled application form if required in the notification. Verify eligibility criteria before attending the interview. Candidates are advised to reach the venue well in advance to complete registration formalities. Keep extra passport-size photographs for submission if required.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to attend the walk-in interview for SAIL DSP recruitment?
faq.answer: The walk-in interviews are scheduled for 21st July 2026 and 22nd July 2026. Candidates should ensure they attend on one of these dates.

faq.question: Q2. What is the maximum age limit for the Consultant posts at SAIL DSP?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for all Consultant posts is 69 years. Specific age relaxation details are not provided in the summary.

faq.question: Q3. Are there any application fees for the SAIL DSP recruitment?
faq.answer: No, there are no application fees mentioned for any category (General/OBC, SC/ST) for this recruitment drive.

faq.question: Q4. What is the selection process for the Consultant posts?
faq.answer: The selection process will involve Document Verification followed by an Interview.

faq.question: Q5. What are the educational qualifications required for the GDMO post?
faq.answer: For the GDMO post, candidates must possess an MBBS degree.

faq.question: Q6. What are the salary ranges for the Specialist posts?
faq.answer: The salary for Specialist posts ranges from Rs. 1,20,000/- to Rs. 1,60,000/- per month, with the Specialist (Surgery) post offering Rs. 1,60,000/- per month.

faq.question: Q7. Where is the walk-in interview for SAIL DSP recruitment being held?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview will be held at the SAIL DSP office in Durgapur, West Bengal, PIN code 713205.

faq.question: Q8. How many vacancies are available for the Consultant (GDMO/Specialist) posts?
faq.answer: There are a total of 11 vacancies available for the Consultant (GDMO/Specialist) positions.

faq.question: Q9. What documents should I carry for the walk-in interview?
faq.answer: You should carry all original documents, including ID proof, educational certificates, experience certificates (if applicable), and category certificates (if applicable), along with one set of self-attested photocopies.

faq.question: Q10. Is this a permanent or contractual job?
faq.answer: The job is on a contractual basis.
